title: Incorrect Binary Exponentiation with Repeated Squaring

statement.markdown: Given two integers $a$ and $b$, implement a function to calculate $a^b$ using efficient binary exponentiation. However, due to a logical error, the function returns incorrect results for certain cases. Fix this issue to obtain accurate results.
statement.notes: Time and space complexity should be optimized.

hints.text: Consider re-examining the handling of edge cases

editorial.title: Correct Binary Exponentiation using Repeated Squaring
editorial.intuition: The function should calculate the power `a^b` by recursively dividing the exponent `b` by 2 and squaring the base `a` when necessary.
editorial.bruteForce: Naively calculating the power using a loop or recursion results in exponential time complexity.
editorial.betterSolution: Utilizing the properties of exponents enables the function to achieve efficient computation.
editorial.optimalSolution: By leveraging the binary representation of the exponent and employing repeated squaring, we can significantly reduce the computational complexity.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the function can be verified by checking its performance on various input cases.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run can help identify potential logical errors.
commonMistakes: Insufficient consideration of binary exponentiation properties

functionSignature.cpp: long long power(long long a, long long b);
functionSignature.python: def power(self, a: int, b: int) -> int:
